pipeline_models._rationale: Cross-model critique. The drafter (Claude Sonnet) and the critic (GPT-5.5) are different models from different vendors. When they agree something is wrong, the signal is much stronger than one model self-critiquing. The decider (Claude Sonnet again) synthesizes the two perspectives. Same pattern gstack uses for /codex review of Claude-authored work.

series_anchor.label: Sanskrit smṛti corpus (Manu, Yājñavalkya, Nārada, Bṛhaspati, Aṅgirasa, Parāśara, Viṣṇu, Yama, Kātyāyana, Vīramitrodaya)
series_anchor.palette: sandalwood, sacred saffron, deep ochre, weathered bronze, palm-leaf cream
series_anchor.mood: Ancient Indian dharma tradition committed to palm leaf. Pre-dawn temple light. The verse-numbered shloka, copied for centuries.
series_anchor.watercolor_treatment: Sandalwood wash as the dominant pigment, allowed to bleed into the paper. Saffron introduced sparingly. A touch of dried-leaf brown for the manuscript edge. Pigment kept thin and allowed to expose the cream paper warmly.

work_metadata.title: Nyaya Tilakam Pandulipi
work_metadata.author: Unknown
work_metadata.description: Sanskrit manuscript on Nyaya philosophy and logic

1_context.content_excerpt:
Shri Raghunath Temple MSS. Library,
JAMMU

| No. | 1566 |
| Title | Nyaya Tilaka [Crown of Logic] |
| Author | Viharacharya |
| Extent | 8 | Age x |
| Subject | Nyayashastra [Science of Logic] |

output.reasoning: The Nyaya Tilakam is a Sanskrit treatise on logic — nyaya means 'rule' or 'method of reasoning,' and the tilaka is a mark of distinction, a crown. A single brass or bronze measuring plumb or balance weight — ancient, worn, used to test and prove — evokes the rigorous logical method without illustrating any scene. It sits within the sandalwood-saffron family of the smṛti series.
output.subject: A single worn bronze plumb weight on a frayed cord, resting at rest
output.palette: Raw umber, yellow ochre, burnt sienna
output.composition: The plumb weight placed in the lower-left third of the composition, the cord trailing upward and dissolving into unpainted cream paper before reaching the top edge; the upper-right two-thirds entirely open warm ground
output.watercolor_treatment: A sandalwood-toned raw umber wet-on-wet wash pooling darkest at the base of the weight and bleeding outward to near-nothing at the paper grain; a single glaze of yellow ochre over the bronze body while still damp, letting both pigments drift and separate; burnt sienna dry-brushed in one or two scuffed strokes along the cord to suggest fiber texture; pencil under-drawing faintly visible at the cord's upper edge; paper grain exposed warmly throughout the negative space
output.prompt: A single worn bronze plumb weight, small and ancient, rests in the lower-left third of warm cream cotton paper, its surface rendered in a wet-on-wet wash of raw umber that pools darkest at its base and bleeds outward until the pigment nearly vanishes into bare paper grain. A glaze of yellow ochre, laid while the umber is still damp, drifts across the body of the weight, the two pigments separating softly at their edges. A frayed cord rises from the weight and dissolves upward into unpainted paper before reaching the top edge, its fiber suggested by two or three dry-brush scuffs of burnt sienna. Pencil under-drawing remains faintly legible along the cord. The upper-right expanse is entirely open, breathing, warm cream — more than half the painting untouched. No hard outlines anywhere.

output.reasoning: The main issue is palette drift: the draft makes raw umber the dominant wash and shifts the support to cotton paper instead of the anchor’s sandalwood / saffron / ochre / bronze / palm-leaf cream system.

issues.problematic_phrase: warm cream cotton paper, its surface rendered in a wet-on-wet wash of raw umber
issues.suggestion: palm-leaf cream paper, its surface rendered in a dominant sandalwood wet-on-wet wash with weathered-bronze depth pooling darkest at its base

output.reasoning: The critic identifies one high-severity issue: palette drift from the series anchor. The original prompt uses 'warm cream cotton paper' and 'raw umber' as the dominant wash, which conflicts with the anchor's sandalwood-dominant palette and palm-leaf cream support. The critic's suggestion to replace these with palm-leaf cream paper and a sandalwood wash with weathered-bronze depth is correct and necessary. The rest of the prompt — the ochre glaze, burnt sienna dry-brush cord, pencil under-drawing, open upper-right composition, no hard outlines — is consistent with the anchor and should be preserved. The fix is surgical: replace the paper descriptor and the dominant wash pigment only.

decisions.reason: Raw umber as the dominant wash and cotton paper as the support both conflict with the series anchor's sandalwood-dominant palette and palm-leaf cream ground, so the replacement is necessary.

output.final_prompt: A single worn bronze plumb weight, small and ancient, rests in the lower-left third of palm-leaf cream paper, its surface rendered in a wet-on-wet wash of sandalwood that pools darkest with weathered-bronze depth at its base and bleeds outward until the pigment nearly vanishes into bare paper grain. A glaze of yellow ochre, laid while the sandalwood wash is still damp, drifts across the body of the weight, the two pigments separating softly at their edges. A frayed cord rises from the weight and dissolves upward into unpainted paper before reaching the top edge, its fiber suggested by two or three dry-brush scuffs of burnt sienna. Pencil under-drawing remains faintly legible along the cord. The upper-right expanse is entirely open, breathing, warm cream — more than half the painting untouched. No hard outlines anywhere.
output.changes_summary: Replaced 'warm cream cotton paper' with 'palm-leaf cream paper' and shifted the dominant wet-on-wet wash from raw umber to sandalwood with weathered-bronze depth pooling at the base, bringing the prompt into alignment with the series anchor palette.

final_prompt: A single worn bronze plumb weight, small and ancient, rests in the lower-left third of palm-leaf cream paper, its surface rendered in a wet-on-wet wash of sandalwood that pools darkest with weathered-bronze depth at its base and bleeds outward until the pigment nearly vanishes into bare paper grain. A glaze of yellow ochre, laid while the sandalwood wash is still damp, drifts across the body of the weight, the two pigments separating softly at their edges. A frayed cord rises from the weight and dissolves upward into unpainted paper before reaching the top edge, its fiber suggested by two or three dry-brush scuffs of burnt sienna.
